hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Removes only the callback associated with the provided specific ID; all other callbacks for that event key remain active.
Emits 'show <ID_of_first_remaining_step> previous=<ID_of_removed_step>'. The current navigation state is updated to the newly first step.

context_pointers.vague_reference: match the internal ordering of the 'props' object in the core module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The output order is determined by JavaScript object property enumeration order (which for strings is insertion order at runtime), specifically following the sequence of keys defined in the 'props' dictionary passed to shorthand functions.

context_pointers.vague_reference: trigger when the step index equals the total list length minus one
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This condition is met when the `current` step index is the maximum possible index in the steps array, indicating all other steps have been visited or the list has only this one step remaining.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_event_system.json.description: A reusable publish/subscribe notifier. Callers register named callbacks against string event keys, then fire an event to invoke every callback currently registered for that key, in registration order, forwarding the event's extra argument to each callback. A one-shot registration runs at most once and is then automatically discarded. Unregistering by event key alone removes every callback for that key; unregistering by event key plus a specific callback removes only that callback. Firing an event with no registered callbacks does nothing. Each case is a sequence of operations; for every fire operation the scenario reports the event name and the ordered list of callbacks that ran (with the value each received), or `(none)` when nothing ran.

feature2_attach_target.json.description: Normalizes a step's attach-target specification into a canonical form with two fields: the target element selector and the placement side. The specification may be supplied either as a single shorthand string of the form `<selector> <placement>` or as an object that already carries both fields. For the string form, the placement must be one of the recognized sides (auto, top, left, right, bottom), optionally suffixed with `-start` or `-end`; the leading portion is taken as the selector. A string whose trailing token is not a recognized placement is rejected. For the object form, the object is accepted as-is only when it carries both an element and a placement; otherwise it is rejected. Accepted specifications report the element selector and placement; rejected ones report `none`.

feature3_shorthand.json.description: Expands a shorthand option value into a keyed object given an ordered list of field names. When the value is a space-separated string, its whitespace-separated tokens are zipped positionally onto the provided field names, producing an object that maps each field name to its corresponding token. When the value is already an object, it is returned unchanged. When the value is null it stays null, and when the value is absent (undefined) it stays undefined. Each case reports the resulting fields as `name=value` lines in the requested field order, or the literal `null` / `undefined` for the pass-through cases.

feature4_1_navigation.json.description: Sequential forward/backward navigation across an ordered list of steps. Starting the tour activates and shows the first step. Advancing moves to the next step in order; going back moves to the previous step. Each transition fires a show notification carrying the step being shown and the step that was previously current (or `none` at the start). The scenario interleaves navigation commands with queries of the currently-shown step, reporting each show notification as `show <current> previous=<previous>` and each query as `current=<id>`.

cases.expected_output:
show a previous=none
current=a
show b previous=a
current=b
show a previous=b
current=a

feature4_2_conditional_skip.json.description: Conditional step skipping during navigation. A step may declare a visibility predicate; when that predicate evaluates to false at navigation time, the step is bypassed and navigation continues to the following step in the travel direction. Steps without a predicate are always eligible. The scenario places a skipped (predicate-false) step between two eligible steps and advances forward, reporting each shown step as `show <current> previous=<previous>` and each query as `current=<id>` so the skipped step never appears as current.

cases.expected_output:
show a previous=none
current=a
show b previous=a
current=b

feature4_3_complete_on_last.json.description: Advancing past the final step finishes the tour. When the currently-shown step is the last in the list, the advance command does not move to another step; instead it completes the tour, which fires a completion notification followed by an inactivation notification and deactivates the tour. The scenario navigates to the last step and then advances once more, reporting show notifications as `show <current> previous=<previous>`, the lifecycle notifications as `complete` and `inactive`, and current-step queries as `current=<id>`.

cases.expected_output:
show a previous=none
show b previous=a
current=b
complete
inactive
active=false

feature4_4_unknown_step.json.description: Requesting a step by a key that does not match any step is a no-op. When a show command names an unknown step, no show notification fires and the currently-shown step is left unchanged. The scenario starts the tour, requests a non-existent step, and verifies via current-step queries that the current step did not change and that no extra show notification was emitted; show notifications are reported as `show <current> previous=<previous>` and queries as `current=<id>`.

feature5_1_add_get.json.description: Adding steps to a tour and retrieving them by identifier. Steps are appended to the tour's ordered collection and can later be looked up by their identifier, returning the matching step (from which its title can be read) or nothing when no step matches. New steps can be added after construction and immediately become part of the collection and retrievable. The scenario reports the collection size as `count=<n>`, and each lookup as `byId <id> title=<title>` (or `title=missing` when no step matches).

cases.expected_output:
count=2
byId welcome title=Welcome
byId details title=Details
byId nope title=missing
count=3
byId finish title=Finish

feature5_2_remove.json.description: Removing steps from a tour by identifier. Removing a step that is not currently shown simply drops it from the collection, shrinking the size by one and leaving the currently-shown step unchanged. Removing the step that is currently shown drops it and, while other steps remain, automatically shows the first remaining step. The scenario removes a non-current step and then the current step, reporting the collection size as `count=<n>`, each current-step query as `current=<id>`, and each automatic transition as `show <current> previous=<previous>`.

cases.expected_output:
show a previous=none
current=a
count=2
current=a
show c previous=none
current=c
count=1

_constraints.input_condition: op is 'on' with event key and unique id
_constraints.output_rule: Callback registered to the event key list; no immediate output

_constraints.input_condition: op is 'once' with event key and id, same key as an existing registration or triggered immediately
_constraints.output_rule: Callback registered once. If the key was already registered (or triggered), the callback is discarded. Output occurs only on the first trigger. Subsequent triggers produce (none).

_constraints.input_condition: op is 'trigger' on an event with registered callbacks
trigger op has 'arg' property
_constraints.output_rule: In output list, each callback id is followed by '=arg' in order of registration

_constraints.input_condition: trigger op has no 'arg' property
_constraints.output_rule: In output list, each callback id stands alone without append

_constraints.input_condition: op is 'off' with event key only
_constraints.output_rule: Remove ALL callbacks for that event key. No output unless immediately followed by a trigger with no callbacks remaining.

_constraints.input_condition: op is 'off' with event key and specific id
_constraints.output_rule: Remove ONLY the specified callback. Others remain active.

_constraints.input_condition: spec is string where last token is NOT in {auto, top-left, top-right, top, left-end, left, right-end, right, bottom-end, bottom}
spec is string where last token IS in {auto, top-left, top-right, top, left-end, left, right-end, right, bottom-end, bottom}
spec is object missing ANY of {element, on}
spec is object with BOTH {element, on}
value is undefined (key missing or null value)
value is explicitly null
value is an object
_constraints.output_rule: Output fields of object line-by-line in 'name=value' format matching 'props' order

_constraints.input_condition: value is space-separated string
_constraints.output_rule: Split string by whitespace. Map nth token to props[n]. Output 'name=value' per prop order

_constraints.input_condition: Navigation changes current step (start, next, back, removeStep)
Navigation targets step that is skipped (showOn=false) or byId on unknown step
_constraints.output_rule: NO show notification is emitted. Current step remains unchanged.

_constraints.input_condition: op is 'current'
op is 'isActive'
op is 'start'
_constraints.output_rule: Set isActive=true, show first step in list. If list empty, isActive=true, current=none.

_constraints.input_condition: op is 'show' with 'key'
_constraints.output_rule: If key exists and is NOT skipped, set current, emit show notification. If key missing or skipped, do nothing (no show)

_constraints.input_condition: current step is the LAST step in list; op is 'next'
Triggered immediately after C022 (conceptually)
current step is NOT last; op is 'next'
current step is NOT first; op is 'back'
current step IS first; op is 'back'
_constraints.output_rule: No change. No show notification. current remains same. isActive remains true.

_constraints.input_condition: op is 'addStep'
_constraints.output_rule: Append step to end of list. current stays same (or becomes last if tour was active and we ended? No, tour stays active). Output count=n.

_constraints.input_condition: Remove an ID different from current; list shrinks by 1
Remove the CURRENT step AND it becomes the last step (list becomes empty or smaller than 1 element)
Remove the CURRENT step and it was the ONLY step (list becomes empty)
_constraints.output_rule: Current stays 'none'. isActive 'false' on next isActive check?

_constraints.input_condition: step has 'showOn: false'
_constraints.output_rule: Step is treated as 'skipped' regardless of navigation direction. Never emits 'show' for it.

_constraints.input_condition: trigger event with no registered callbacks

hide.replacement: a specific ID to remove one callback
exact_values_to_replace: op is 'off' with event key and specific id
hide.oracle_response: Removes only the callback associated with the provided specific ID; all other callbacks for that event key remain active.

hide.replacement: a specific remaining element ID after removal
hide.oracle_response: Emits 'show <ID_of_first_remaining_step> previous=<ID_of_removed_step>'. The current navigation state is updated to the newly first step.
